channel.title: Cybersecurity News Aggregator
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity news from top sources
channel.comment: News aggregated from: Krebs on Security, The Hacker News, Threatpost, Bleeping Computer, Dark Reading

item.title: SonicWall warns of SMA1000 flaws exploited in zero-day attacks, patch now
item.description: SonicWall warns that threat actors have been exploiting two SMA1000 vulnerabilities, tracked as CVE-2026-15409 and CVE-2026-15410, in zero-day attacks and urges customers to install the newly released...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft Patches Record 622 Flaws, Including Two Zero-Days Under Active Attack
item.description: Microsoft shipped its largest Patch Tuesday on record today, and two of the fixes close holes that attackers are already exploiting. The release covers 622 of Microsoft's own CVEs by its Security Upda...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Spanish Police take down €140 million cyber fraud ring, arrest four
item.description: The Spanish Police dismantled a cybercrime and money-laundering organization that made €140 million ($160 million) from investment fraud and business email compromise (BEC) attacks. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 6 GHz Wi-Fi Flaws Could Disrupt Critical Systems
item.description: Automated Frequency Coordination systems by default trust client-side data, which could lead to location spoofing and other attacks that disrupt traffic....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Microsoft Patches a Record 570 Security Flaws
item.description: Microsoft Corp. today released software updates to plug at least 570 security holes in its Windows operating systems and other software, almost triple the number of vulnerabilities the software giant ...
item.dc:creator: Krebs on Security

item.title: Nearly 300 GitHub repos pose as legit software to push malware
item.description: A threat actor has published hundreds of fake GitHub repositories impersonating legitimate software and security projects to distribute infostealer malware. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Microsoft releases Windows 10 KB5099539 extended security update
item.description: Microsoft has released the Windows 10 KB5099539 extended security update, which includes the July 2026 Patch Tuesday security updates for 570 vulnerabilities, along with additional security fixes. [.....
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: SAP Patches CVSS 9.9 NetWeaver ABAP Flaw That Could Expose or Modify Data
item.description:
SAP has rolled out updates to address multiple vulnerabilities as part of its July 2026 security updates, including a critical flaw in SAP NetWeaver Application Server ABAP.
The vulnerability in ques...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft July 2026 Patch Tuesday fixes massive 570 flaws, 3 zero-days
item.description: Today is Microsoft's July 2026 Patch Tuesday, and with it comes security updates for a record-breaking 570 flaws, including two zero-day vulnerabilities exploited in attacks and one publicly disclosed...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Manage Vendor Risk in a Few Practical Steps
item.description: Risk tolerance, exposure visibility, board oversight — handling third-party risk is complicated but achievable with disciplined, precise governance....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Windows 11 KB5101650 & KB5099414 cumulative updates released
item.description: Microsoft has released Windows 11 KB5101650 and KB5099414 cumulative updates for versions 25H2/24H2 and 23H2 to fix security vulnerabilities, bugs, and add new features. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Researchers Say Claude for Chrome Flaw Lets Rogue Extensions Trigger Gmail Reads
item.description:
Any other browser extension that can run a script on claude.ai can still trigger Claude for Chrome tasks aimed at your Gmail, your latest Google Doc and its comments, and your Calendar.
Both this and...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: LabubaRAT Masquerades as NVIDIA Software to Control Windows Hosts
item.description:
Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a previously undocumented Rust-based remote access trojan (RAT) codenamed LabubaRAT that masquerades as NVIDIA software to blend into target environments.
"Labu...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Progress confirms ShareFile zero-day flaw behind Storage Zone shutdown
item.description: Progress Software has confirmed that a high-severity zero-day vulnerability is behind the emergency shutdown of ShareFile Storage Zone Controllers last week and has released security updates to patch ...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Frontier AI: The Genie's Out of the Bottle, but Where's the Rulebook?
item.description: Cutting-edge artificial intelligence models are deploying with more independence and less human oversight. Several state governments are trying to legislate transparency in their use....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: ClickFix's Mushrooming Ecosystem Demands New Defense Tactics
item.description: The attack vector is available for rent at scale, and evades AV and EDR, leaving YARA analysis as the best detection option....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: LastPass, Bitwarden users targeted with fake security alerts
item.description: LastPass is warning users about an ongoing phishing campaign that is using fake security notices to direct them to fraudulent websites. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: You Don't Have to Run an Exploit to Know If You're Vulnerable
item.description: Many vulnerabilities cannot be safely validated with live exploits, either because no exploit exists or the affected systems are too critical to test. Picus explains how TTP chaining helps organizatio...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: RabbitMQ Flaws Could Leak OAuth Secrets and Expose Cross-Tenant Queue Metadata
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of two access control-related flaws impacting the RabbitMQ message broker service that could allow attackers to leak OAuth client secrets, expose enter...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Cursor IDE Auto-Executes Malicious Code in Poisoned Repos
item.description: Researchers reported the vulnerability to Cursor in December, but it still remains in the popular AI coding platform and can be exploited in poisoned repository attacks....
item.dc:creator: Dark Reading

item.title: Microsoft Entra ID gets passkeys default authentication starting September
item.description: Microsoft has announced that passkeys will become the default authentication method for the Entra ID enterprise identity service starting September 2026. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: New phishing kits target Microsoft 365 accounts, evade MFA
item.description: Two new phishing kits, Jalisco and OmegaLord, have been discovered in attacks targeting Microsoft 365 accounts, using techniques that defeat multi-factor authentication (MFA). [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: 11 Old Microsoft-Signed Linux UEFI Shims Could Let Attackers Bypass Secure Boot
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered 11 old, Microsoft-signed, Unified Extensible Firmware Interface (UEFI) applications that could be abused to bypass Secure Boot on most systems using the moder...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Study of 85 Crypto Wallet Extensions Finds Address Leaks and Cross-Site Tracking Risks
item.description:
Researchers at KU Leuven tested 85 of the most popular crypto wallets that run as browser extensions and found that the wallets themselves leak enough to link and track the people using them.
The way...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: SAP warns of critical flaws in NetWeaver and Commerce Cloud
item.description: SAP has addressed 16 vulnerabilities across multiple products as part of its July 2026 security updates, including three critical flaws in NetWeaver, Commerce Cloud, and AppRouter. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: How Pentera Turns AI Security Workflows into Validation Engines
item.description: AI security agents are starting to influence real security decisions. They summarize findings, prioritize remediation, recommend next steps, and help teams move faster. But most still rely on fragment...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: OAuth Client ID Spoofing Lets Attackers Validate Stolen Microsoft Entra Credentials
item.description:
At least two distinct threat actors are weaponizing a novel evasion technique called OAuth client ID spoofing in cloud campaigns, while slipping past telemetry.
The activity allows users to enumerate...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News

item.title: Microsoft starts testing cleaner Windows Search without ads
item.description: Microsoft is now testing a cleaner and faster version of Windows Search that should prioritize relevant results over ads and promotional content. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: US sanctions VPN, malware providers for enabling ransomware attacks
item.description: The U.S. Treasury Department's Office of Foreign Assets Control (OFAC) sanctioned two individuals and one entity for enabling ransomware attacks against U.S. organizations. [...]...
item.dc:creator: Bleeping Computer

item.title: Grok Build Uploaded Entire Git Repositories to xAI Storage, Not Just Files It Read
item.description:
xAI's Grok Build coding CLI was uploading entire Git repositories, full commit history and all, to a Google Cloud Storage bucket run by xAI, not just the files a coding task needed.
A researcher publ...
item.dc:creator: The Hacker News
